features.name: Environment & Data Download
features.description: Verify uv environment installs, data download script works, and basic AnnData reads succeed on the DE stats file
features.evidence: Env imports OK (anndata 0.13.1, mudata 0.3.10, h5py 3.16.0). Aggregate layer downloaded and verified: data/raw ~101 GB — 4 HDF5 (DE 16.8 / pseudobulk 44.6 / by_guide 29.4 / by_donors 16.9 GB), 15 suppl tables, 12 Croissant jsonld. README download steps extended: S3 sync for on-S3 suppl+metadata, GitHub fetch for the 12 analysis tables not on S3. init.sh passes (compileall clean). Backed AnnData reads confirmed via examples/inspect_de_stats.py. Cell-level files intentionally excluded (storage-blocked).

features.name: Data Inspection & ID Mapping
features.description: Load DE stats and pseudobulk AnnData/MuData objects, extract obs/var tables, build Ensembl-HGNC-UniProt-Entrez identifier mapping with ambiguity reporting
features.evidence: Inspection DONE (examples/ inspectors). ID mapping DONE: src/tcell_pipeline/id_mapping.py extracts unique Ensembl from backed DE .obs[target_contrast] + .var[gene_ids] (never loads layers), maps Ensembl->HGNC offline via obs/var crossref and Ensembl->UniProt/Entrez via mygene.info. Ran online on real DE file: 12311 unique Ensembl (11526 targets, 10282 measured, 9497 both), all HGNC resolved, UniProt/Entrez filled. One-to-many UniProt DISAMBIGUATED (choose_uniprot): candidate accessions restricted to the gene's reviewed human canonical (UniProt REST gene_exact+reviewed), picked by annotation-score then lexical; uniprot_ambiguous marks ONLY equal-evidence ties (top score not strictly > runner-up). 33 multi-accession genes -> 23 resolved to a confident canonical (15 single reviewed e.g. CALM1/2/3 -> P0DP23/24/25, + 8 score-decisive where a fragment/secondary loses on annotation score), 10 genuine equal-evidence multi-product loci (CDKN2A p16/p14ARF, GNAS, MOCS2 bicistronic, TMPO isoforms) flagged uniprot_ambiguous with all candidates kept in uniprot_alternatives. Kept the gene as the perturbation unit (CRISPRi knocks down the whole locus); no forced single-protein pick for true ties. Wrote data/intermediate/id_mapping.parquet (adds uniprot_alternatives, uniprot_ambiguous) + ambiguity_report.txt. Delivered in the Module 0 pipeline (run_module0.py orchestrator). init.sh green (compileall + 20 pytest).

features.name: Leakage-Safe Train/Val/Test Splits
features.description: Construct splits that block gene families, protein complexes, and close graph neighborhoods from leaking between train and test; hash and freeze split files
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/splits.py + docs/specs/2026-07-15-feat-003-leakage-safe-splits.md (design derived from the experiment-plan report: Training Splits, Phase-1 Split Lock, Experimental Units/Partitions). Empirical measurement on the real marts (11525 target genes) forced the algorithm: naive connected-components collapses on every axis (physical_ppi 1-hop -> 95% giant component, complex co-membership -> 23%, raw ESM cosine>=0.95 -> 92%, centered single-link -> 15%, Louvain -> 42%). Hard block = sequence/paralog family via REPRESENTATIVE (non-chaining, CD-HIT-style) clustering on CENTERED ESM-2 embeddings at cosine>=0.85 (measured largest family 3.1%), merged with direct CORUM complex co-membership under a 5%-of-genes size cap (capped union-find refuses giant merges: 3986 refused on real data). Physical-PPI neighbourhood is AUDIT-ONLY, not a partition constraint (per report G1 gate + Phase-1 step 5/6/9: 'cluster sequence/paralog similarity, separately stress PPI neighbourhoods, publish the similarity distribution'). 4-role partition per report (train/val/calibration/challenge ~60/15/10/15) assigned by whole family group, seeded, deficit-greedy; realized on real data 62.5/13.0/7.9/16.6%. Random diagnostic split (row-level) as the sanity baseline. Frozen + hashed to data/splits/ (git-tracked): blocked_target_ood.csv, random.csv, manifest.json (seed/thresholds/cap/sha256/realized fractions), leakage_report.json (machine-readable audit: hard-asserts no family group split across roles, publishes per-axis train->challenge residual). Effectiveness validated: blocking cuts the fraction of challenge genes with a >=0.85 train paralog from 53.5% (random) to 28.1% (blocked) = 47% reduction. config: SPLITS_ROOT/SPLIT_ROLES/SPLIT_FRACTIONS/SPLIT_SEED/SEQ_SIM_COSINE_THRESHOLD/GROUP_SIZE_CAP/SPLIT_AUDIT_HOPS + artifact paths. 8 synthetic tests (src/tests/test_splits.py): paralog+complex grouping, size-cap refusal, no family split across roles, seed determinism, singleton distribution, leakage-audit pass + fail-closed on injected split, random-split fractions, grouping ignores non-q_pre columns. init.sh green (compileall + 54 pytest). ponytail: centered-cosine threshold + cap are calibration knobs (report defers tuning to the published distribution); physical block is audit-only by data necessity; curated-family download and pairwise-must-link residual reduction are documented upgrade paths. UPDATE (2026-07-15, post code-review; split CSVs byte-identical, sha256 unchanged): three leakage-audit bugs fixed in splits.py -- (1) audit now publishes cap-induced family splits via an uncapped pre-cap component pass (the post-cap 'no split' assertion was blind to families the 5% cap must break: cap_induced_family_splits=1, family_challenge_sharing_train_frac=0.41, an upper bound inflated by single-linkage chaining); (2) sequence residual now centers train+challenge in one global frame (was per-subset means -> mismatched frames understating similarity); (3) run() fails closed when PLM embeddings are absent instead of silently publishing a sequence-leaky split as safe (override via SPLITS_ALLOW_NO_SEQUENCE). manifest gains sequence_block_active + n_genes_with_embedding. Corrected effectiveness: 53.8% (random) -> 26.4% (blocked) = 51% reduction (supersedes the 53.5/28.1/47 figures above). Tier-3: diagnostic random split (assign_random) now uses cumulative-boundary allocation (last boundary == n, no truncated tail) -- random.csv regenerated; blocked_target_ood.csv byte-identical to the 35e3999 freeze and effectiveness numbers unchanged (gene-level baseline doesn't shift at N=11525). Dead config SPLIT_AUDIT_HOPS removed. +1 regression test (random split covers all items at small N). See docs/reviews/2026-07-15-code-review-feat-016-feat-003.md.

features.name: PPI Graph Construction
features.description: Download and harmonize typed protein interaction edges from BioPlex, HuRI, BioGRID, STRING, and CORUM with full provenance; build merged heterogeneous graph table
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/ppi_graph.py implements the typed-edge harmonizer (undirected canonicalization, uppercase, per-(pair,source) dedup, n_supporting_sources, is_physical/is_functional/is_complex/is_direct_binary flags, score clipped to [0,1], missing score floored to 0 not 1) + best-effort downloaders for all 5 sources (log-and-continue, cache in data/raw/ppi/<source>/; BioGRID multi-member-ZIP handled, HuRI extended via mygene when online with drop logging). complex_membership.py parses CORUM into bipartite membership. ppi_degree_* now computed from the graph into perturbation_condition (ppi_graph runs before perturbation_table). Harmonizer + degree + CORUM-schema unit-tested (test_ppi_graph.py, test_perturbation_table.py, test_complex_membership.py). RUN ON REAL SOURCES: all 5 fetch+integrate -> protein_edges.parquet with 7,980,907 edges (bioplex 118162, huri 52256, biogrid 1218142, string 13715404, corum 77696); complex_membership 18932 memberships / 5628 complexes. Fixes this session: HuRI URL apex host (cert invalid for www); CORUM migrated to CORUM 5.3 fastapi endpoint (fastapi-corum/public/file/download_current_file?file_id=human&file_format=txt, new subunits_gene_name schema, per-source TLS-verify skip for the broken helmholtz cert chain).

features.name: Latent Program Extraction
features.description: Fit fold-local program bases (sparse PCA, NMF, ICA, shallow VAE) at 64/128/256/512 dims on training data only; compare on reconstruction, sparsity, and stability
features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-15): fold-local program-basis extraction machinery landed in src/tcell_pipeline/programs/ (program_basis.py: fit_program_basis + train_row_indices fold-locality gate + save/load; run_program_basis.py orchestrator). Methods sparse_pca (MiniBatchSparsePCA, default) / nmf / fastica (ICA) / svd; K configurable (config.PROGRAM_DIM=128; §6.5 sweep 64/128/256/512). Fits on TRAIN rows only (21262 real rows x 10282 genes) with a challenge-overlap assert. Validated: 12 synthetic tests (test_programs.py) + real run_program_basis --method svd (6.2s) wrote gene_program_loadings.parquet (B 10282x128) and program_response.parquet (A 21262x128). REMAINING for done: 4-method x 4-K comparison metrics (reconstruction / sparsity / stability) and a shallow-VAE basis. UPDATE (2026-07-15, full real-data verification pass): ran the paper-default PRODUCTION fit `run_program_basis --method sparse_pca` on the real train fold in 289s -> re-wrote gene_program_loadings.parquet (B 10282x128) + program_response.parquet (A 21262x128), overwriting the earlier svd smoke output as the frozen production loadings (gitignored). Validated: B/A all finite; fold-locality EXACT (saved A row_index set == the 21262 train rows); loadings 22.7% exact-zero (SparsePCA sparsity at default alpha=1, no dead programs); centered reconstruction MAE 0.687 vs 0.817 predict-zero baseline (16% explained — sparse_pca trades reconstruction for sparsity vs svd ~0.61, as expected). splits.py re-run byte-identical (all 4 sha256 == frozen freeze). Status stays in-progress: the method x K comparison metrics + shallow-VAE basis still remain.

features.name: Simple Baselines
features.description: Implement no-effect, condition-mean, ridge, elastic-net, nearest-neighbor, low-rank, and CatBoost baselines with common output schema

features.name: Graph Baselines
features.description: Implement network propagation, untyped GNN, and typed static graph baselines using the PPI graph from feat-004

features.name: EG-IPG Model Implementation
features.description: Implement the full EG-IPG architecture: perturbation encoder, typed condition-gated graph encoder, program decoder with residual pathway, and sparse predictive-rationale head
features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-15): Module 3 program decoder + full M1+M2+M3 wiring scaffolded (delivered with the feat-005 Module-3 goal). src/tcell_pipeline/programs/program_decoder.py (ProgramDecoder: graph path Linear(512,K) + expression-only path Linear(256,K), sigmoid mixture gate lambda in [0,1], softplus uncertainty sigma, gene decode delta_x = B @ delta_z^T + r with B a FROZEN registered_buffer, not a Parameter). src/tcell_pipeline/model.py (EGIPGModel wraps PerturbationEncoder + TypedGraphEncoder + ProgramDecoder; graph_encoder=None yields the expression-only nested variant with lambda pinned to 0). Real-data smoke run_module3_smoke.py PASSED end-to-end (M1->M2->M3 on 4 real perturbations: shapes ok, finite, lambda in [0,1], sigma>0). REMAINING: Module 4 sparse predictive-rationale head, training losses, and train/calibration loops. UPDATE (2026-07-16): Module 4 sparse PREDICTIVE-rationale head built (Stage B — fitted AFTER the H1 predictor freeze; a predictive rationale, NOT a causal mechanism, deletion scores are fixed-model perturbation tests). New package src/tcell_pipeline/rationale/: RationaleHead (edge importance imp = alpha_bar * sigmoid(Linear([h_u||h_v||f_e])), zero-init scorer so an untrained head ranks by the frozen condition gate = faithful by construction; top-k selection across all 4 relations; output labelled 'predictive_rationale', never 'causal'); RationaleLoss (sparsity |S| + sufficiency ||dz_S - dz_full||^2 + necessity hinge on ||dz_\S - dz_full|| + contrastive vs matched-random; differentiable to the head via soft gate weights); FaithfulnessTester (fixed-model deletion tests: sufficiency/necessity re-run the FROZEN encoder with the rationale kept/removed, plus structural_ood_audit reporting degree/components/sparsity/hop-distance before-vs-after); MatchedRandomSampler (controls matched on per-relation edge count -> size + relation composition). Module-2 enabler: TypedGraphEncoder.encode_subgraph(...) added to expose final node states + accept a per-edge gate mask; encode_one unchanged (delegates to it, 3-tuple contract preserved). config: RATIONALE_TOP_K=15, RATIONALE_TAU=0.5, LAMBDA_SPARSE=0.01/LAMBDA_SUFF=1.0/LAMBDA_NEC=1.0/LAMBDA_CONTRAST=0.5, N_MATCHED_CONTROLS=100. Verified: 9 new synthetic tests (src/tests/test_rationale.py: imp in [0,1], top-k sorted, sufficiency<matched-random, necessity>matched-random, matched-random size+relation match, structural_ood dict, loss components computable+gradients, expr-only (no graph)->empty rationale, label 'predictive_rationale' not 'causal'); ./init.sh green at 78 tests (69 prior + 9 new). Real-data smoke run_module4_smoke.py PASSED on the real PPI graph (A1BG neighbourhood: sufficiency<matched-random, necessity>matched-random, labelled predictive_rationale). Perf: this 64-core box thrashes torch's thread pool on tiny per-subgraph GNN ops (2.5s->20ms/encode), so the CPU-only Module-4 tests + smoke pin torch.set_num_threads(1). REMAINING for feat-008 done: the training-loss OPTIMIZATION loop + train/calibration loops (the loss module exists, no fit loop yet); feat-007 (graph baselines) is still not-started.

features.name: Model Screening & Ablations

features.name: Predictive-Rationale Audit

features.name: Perturbation & Context Encoder (Module 1)
features.description: Encode each (perturbation, context) into h_do in R^256 from q_pre features only: frozen PLM+PINNACLE target embeddings + PPI degree + control baseline, trainable culture-condition embedding, donor PCs via Linear (no free donor-ID embedding), guide-quality features; leakage fence rejects q_post at the module boundary. Component of feat-008, built ahead since it depends only on Module 0 outputs.
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/encoders/ package. PluggableEmbeddingStore: frozen PLM (1280) / PINNACLE (512) lookup by UniProt accession, in-memory cache, zero-vector fallback when the parquet is absent (embeddings not computed yet), validates embedding dim; NOT an nn.Module. TargetEncoder: NO trainable gene-ID embedding (H1 prohibited); h_target in R^1796 = PLM 1280 + PINNACLE 512 + ppi_degree_physical/functional/complex + control_baseline_expr. ContextEncoder: trainable nn.Embedding(3,64) for Rest/Stim8hr/Stim48hr + donor_pc_00..31 through Linear(32,32) (NO free donor-ID embedding, so leave-one-donor-out stays valid); h_context in R^96. QualityEncoder: n_guides + single_guide_estimate + zeros(64) guide-seq placeholder; h_quality in R^66. PerturbationEncoder: forward(batch_dict) -> (B,256); fusion Linear(1958->256) + LayerNorm; rejects any q_post column at the boundary (ValueError). CPU-only, batch-first, 503,264 trainable params. config: added PLM_EMBED_DIM/PINNACLE_EMBED_DIM/GUIDE_SEQ_EMBED_DIM/H_DO_DIM/CONDITIONS/PLM_EMBEDDINGS_PATH/PINNACLE_EMBEDDINGS_PATH. NaN guard: as_float_vector nan_to_num neutralises missing control_baseline_expr (1558/33983 rows) and n_guides so one NaN cannot poison the LayerNorm'd h_do (ponytail: upgrade to fold-fit imputation in the Module 3 loader). Verified: 10 tests in src/tests/test_encoders.py (zero-fallback, dims, distinct condition embeddings, no-NaN batch, only the 3-way condition Embedding exists, q_post rejected, missing-scalar guard) + real-data smoke feeding perturbation_condition/de_obs head through the encoder -> finite (4,256) with PLM/PINNACLE absent. init.sh green (compileall + 33 pytest).

features.name: Real PLM + PINNACLE Embedding Ingestion (Module 1)
features.description: Populate the frozen target-embedding stores feat-014 left as zero-fallback: generate real ESM-2 650M PLM vectors and map real PINNACLE cell-type-contextualised vectors onto the mart's UniProt IDs, so the PerturbationEncoder runs on real target embeddings. Corrects the PINNACLE dim placeholder.
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/embeddings_plm.py: real ESM-2 650M (t33) per-protein embeddings (1280-d, mean-pooled over final-layer residues, BOS/EOS/pad excluded). Sequences fetched from UniProt REST accessions endpoint (cached to uniprot_sequences.parquet); resumable (skips embedded proteins, atomic checkpoint) writing config.PLM_EMBEDDINGS_PATH. Device-aware: runs on GPU when available. Ran on an A100 (torch 2.13.0+cu126) -> 11419/11419 mart proteins embedded, all finite (100% PLM coverage). src/tcell_pipeline/embeddings_pinnacle.py: real PINNACLE (Li et al. 2024, Figshare article 22708126) contextual protein embeddings. Discovered the real embedding dim is 128 (config placeholder was 512 -> CORRECTED to 128). Picked the 'cd4-positive helper t cell' context (the screen's cell type; configurable via config.PINNACLE_CONTEXT); PINNACLE keys proteins by gene symbol, mapped to UniProt via id_mapping.hgnc_symbol -> 1119 embeddings written to config.PINNACLE_EMBEDDINGS_PATH, covering 1070/11419 mart proteins (contextual embeddings only span in-network proteins; rest keep the zero fallback). config: PINNACLE_EMBED_DIM 512->128; added PINNACLE_RAW_DIR/PINNACLE_FIGSHARE_URL/PINNACLE_CONTEXT. Live encoder dims now derive to target.out_dim=1412 (1280+128+4), fusion Linear(1574->256), 404,960 trainable params. Tests rewritten to use REAL data/embeddings, no synthetic parquets (10 tests in src/tests/test_encoders.py): real PLM parquet present-loaded + absent-id zero-fallback + dim-mismatch guard, real PINNACLE CD4-context load, forward/NaN tests on the real perturbation_condition + de_obs marts. requirements.txt: +fair-esm, +cu126 torch install note for this host's CUDA-12.2 driver. Artifacts (plm/pinnacle_embeddings.parquet) are gitignored under data/intermediate, regenerable via `python -m tcell_pipeline.embeddings_{plm,pinnacle}`. init.sh green (compileall + 37 pytest).

features.name: Typed Graph Encoder (Module 2)
features.description: Condition-gated signed relational GNN over the typed PPI graph: build a PyG HeteroData (protein + complex nodes; physical_ppi/co_complex/functional_assoc/complex_membership relations), sample a bounded neighbourhood per perturbation target, run 3-layer message passing where messages are signed (tanh*relu) and gated per condition, and cross-attend h_do over the node states to produce h_graph in R^256 plus per-relation edge gates for Module 4. Component of feat-008, built ahead since it depends only on Module 0 outputs + Module 1's h_do.
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/graph/ package (torch_geometric 2.8). graph_builder.build_hetero_graph -> (HeteroData, gene_to_idx): protein nodes keyed by upper-case HGNC (25440 on real data) carrying the same frozen 1412-d descriptor as Module 1's TargetEncoder (PLM 1280 + PINNACLE 128 + 3 graph-derived degrees + control_baseline_expr, zero-fallback via PluggableEmbeddingStore); complex nodes (5628, index-only, learned nn.Embedding in the encoder); 4 relations split by is_physical/is_complex/is_functional flags + bipartite complex_membership, each with an 8-d edge feature (source one-hot(5)|score|is_direct_binary|n_supporting). Real edge counts: physical_ppi 1123205, co_complex 48389, functional_assoc 6857702, complex_membership 18932. neighborhood_sampler.sample_subgraph: grows physical/co-complex first then fills by score, caps at NEIGHBORHOOD_CAP=512 protein nodes, pulls in member complexes, returns an induced HeteroData preserving orig_idx. typed_graph_encoder.TypedGraphEncoder(nn.Module): projects protein features + complex embeddings to 256-d, 3 GRAPH_LAYERS of per-relation custom MessagePassing (W_r/U_r/W_sign/W_mag) with signed message m=tanh(W_sign h_u)*relu(W_mag h_u) and residual FFN+LayerNorm per node type; condition gate alpha=sigmoid(w_gate[h_cond(64)||f_e(8)]) computed once per relation (layer-independent) and returned as edge_gates for Module 4 (the SAME edge is gated differently per culture condition); DropEdge p=0.1 in training. graph_readout.GraphReadout: 4-head cross-attention (q=h_do, K=V=node states) -> h_graph R^256, attention sums to 1. forward(target_genes, conditions, h_do) loops per-target subgraphs -> (h_graph (B,256), edge_gates dict-of-lists); targets absent from the PPI graph fall back to a zero h_graph. config: GRAPH_HOPS/NEIGHBORHOOD_CAP/GRAPH_HIDDEN_DIM/GRAPH_LAYERS/GRAPH_N_HEADS/EDGE_DROPOUT/EDGE_FEATURE_DIM/N_RELATION_TYPES/COMPLEX_EMBED_DIM/CONDITION_EMBED_DIM/RELATION_TYPES/PROTEIN_FEATURE_DIM. CPU and CUDA (device-aware; subgraphs moved to the module device). Verified: 8 synthetic tests in src/tests/test_graph.py (HeteroData structure, 2-hop cap, condition gate differs, signed-message tanh+relu, forward (4,256) finite, edge_gates per relation, zero-degree/absent target, readout attention sums to 1) + src/tcell_pipeline/graph/run_module2_smoke.py real-data smoke: builds the full 25440-node graph in ~18s, samples the CD3E neighbourhood (512 proteins / 740 complexes), runs real Module 1 h_do -> Module 2 h_graph (4,256) finite on GPU, gates differ across conditions, attention sums to 1. init.sh green (compileall + 46 pytest). UPDATE (2026-07-15, post code-review): fixed graph_builder degree column order to match Module 1 TARGET_SCALAR_KEYS [physical, functional, complex]; encode_one now moves h_do to the module device; edge-feature nan_to_num + gene-symbol dropna + unknown-PPI-source fail-fast; OOV culture_condition raises a legible ValueError (_condition_index, still fail-fast on the closed 3-value vocab); returned edge_gates[rel] is now length E (one per original edge) for all relations aligned to the sub-graph edge_index, not 2E-doubled for PP (full gate->edge identity-forwarding API deferred to Module 4); dead config N_RELATION_TYPES/RELATION_TYPES removed; flaky signed-message test seeded + false |out|<1 bound dropped; +2 regression tests. See docs/reviews/2026-07-15-code-review-feat-016-feat-003.md.
